stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML technologies including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
The mention of 'safetensors' indicates relevance to modern, efficient model deployment practices.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model.
It is categorized under text-generation pipelines, indicating its primary function as a generative AI model.
The associated tags (transformers, safetensors, llama) confirm its relevance within the modern LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: How AI Agents Reshape Knowledge Work: Autonomy, Efficiency, and Scope
stories.author: Jerry Ma
bullet_points: Frontier AI is evolving from conversational assistants to autonomous agents capable of end-to-end task execution.
The research uses production data from Perplexity's Search and Computer products to study this transition.
The study yields three key empirical findings regarding how AI agents accelerate and reshape knowledge work.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation tasks, indicating its primary function as a large language model.
Tags suggest relevance to transformer architectures, safetensors, and conversational AI applications.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating a focus on large language models.
Associated technologies include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors, placing it within the core ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, leveraging Apple's native ML framework.
This preview suggests a significant performance boost for running AI models locally on Apple hardware.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The key feature is running all AI processes entirely on the user's own device for privacy/control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running local or cloud coding tools.
It simplifies setup by eliminating the need for environment variables or config files.
The tool supports integration with various coding models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.

stories.title: School shooting survivor sues AI gun detection firm after system failed to spot weapon
stories.author: Cyrus Farivar
bullet_points: A survivor of a school shooting is suing an AI gun detection firm due to system failure.
The case raises critical questions about the required accuracy and reliability of AI safety systems.
This highlights the real-world, high-stakes implications and ethical challenges of deploying AI in public safety.

stories.title: S&P 500 rejects SpaceX, also blocking entry for OpenAI and Anthropic
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: S&P 500 is reportedly blocking easy access to capital for SpaceX.
The exclusion extends to major AI players like OpenAI and Anthropic.
This suggests a potential shift in how large, high-growth, or speculative tech companies gain passive investment.

stories.title: Act As a Real Researcher: A Suite of Benchmarks Evaluating Frontier LLMs and Agentic Harnesses in Research Lifecycle
stories.author: Xiangyong Cao
bullet_points: New benchmarks evaluate frontier LLMs and agentic harnesses across the entire research lifecycle.
Agents show proficiency in complex coding and autonomous experiment execution, marking a shift from assistants to research agents.
Current limitations in field sensitivity, ethics, and nuanced judgment prevent agents from fully replacing human researchers.

stories.title: Code2LoRA: Hypernetwork-Generated Adapters for Code Language Models under Software Evolution
stories.author: Pengyu Nie
bullet_points: Introduces Code2LoRA, a hypernetwork framework designed to inject repository-level context into Code Language Models (LLMs).
Addresses the limitations of current methods (long inputs/RAG or full fine-tuning) which are costly and brittle to software evolution.
Achieves repository-specific knowledge injection with zero inference-time token overhead by generating LoRA adapters.

stories.title: MLEvolve: A Self-Evolving Framework for Automated Machine Learning Algorithm Discovery
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Introduces MLEvolve, a novel LLM-based framework designed for self-evolving Automated Machine Learning (AutoML) algorithm discovery.
Addresses critical limitations in current MLE agents, specifically inter-branch information isolation, memoryless search, and lack of hierarchical control.
Positions self-evolving capabilities as a key requirement for LLM agents tackling complex, long-horizon tasks like scientific discovery.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes 'local-first' operation, allowing AI to run on user hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machines.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local LLM platforms and proprietary API services.

stories.title: NASA will wear high-tech Prada long johns to the Moon
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: NASA is collaborating with Prada on the Liquid Cooling and Ventilation Garment (LCVG) for Artemis IV missions.
The LCVG serves as the essential base layer worn underneath the Axiom Extravehicular Mobility Unit (AxEMU) spacesuit.
This high-tech garment is slated for use during the return of humans to the Moon in 2028.

stories.title: How a USB-connected speaker can infect a PC without ever being touched
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A USB-connected speaker has been shown to potentially infect a PC without physical interaction.
The specific device mentioned is the Sound Blaster Katana V2X.
The seller of the device does not classify the observed behavior as a security vulnerability.

stories.title: Small modular nuclear reactor reaches criticality in first test
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: A startup named Antares has achieved criticality in a small modular nuclear reactor (SMR).
The reactor is currently in an early testing phase and is not yet capable of generating power.
This development relates to the growing field of advanced nuclear energy technologies.

stories.title: Sparse Subspace-to-Expert Sharing for Task-Agnostic Continual Learning
stories.author: Ali Jannesari
bullet_points: Addresses the plasticity-stability dilemma in Continual Learning for LLMs, a major challenge in AI.
Proposes SETA (Sparse Subspace-to-Expert Sharing for Task-Agnostic Continual Learning) to mitigate catastrophic forgetting.
The framework uses adaptive sparse experts to differentiate between task-specific knowledge and shared model capabilities.

stories.title: Twelve quick tips for designing AI-driven HPC workflows
stories.author: Jamie J. Alnasir
bullet_points: HPC clusters are shifting from traditional deterministic pipelines to new AI-driven workflows.
AI integration introduces a new computational paradigm characterized by iterative, data-driven, and probabilistic processes.
The shift presents unique technical challenges for designing and managing these modern AI-driven HPC workflows.

stories.title: Whisper Hallucination Detection and Mitigation via Hidden Representation Steering and Sparse AutoEncoders
stories.author: Assel Yermekova
bullet_points: Addresses the issue of 'hallucinations' in the widely used Whisper ASR model, where it generates false transcripts.
Proposes detecting and mitigating these hallucinations by analyzing Whisper's internal representations (audio encoder activations).
Evaluates two representation spaces—raw activations and Sparse AutoEncoder (SAE) latents—finding they encode hallucination-related information.

stories.title: Planning-aligned Token Compression for Long-Context Autonomous Driving
stories.author: Marco Pavone
bullet_points: Addresses the computational bottleneck of monolithic vision-action models in autonomous driving when handling long temporal contexts.
Proposes 'Planning-aligned Token Compression' as a method to reduce token sequence size without requiring modifications to the core model architecture.
Highlights that existing compression methods are often rule-based, suggesting a need for more integrated solutions.

stories.title: PaperFlow: Profiling, Recommending, and Adapting Across Daily Paper Streams
stories.author: Cheng Tan
bullet_points: Introduces PaperFlow, a framework designed to address the limitations of static scientific paper recommendation.
The framework operates in three stages: Profiling (building scholarly profiles from diverse data), Recommending, and Adapting.
It models scientific reading as a dynamic, longitudinal process where user interests evolve over time.

stories.title: TEVI: Text-Conditioned Editing of Visual Representations via Sparse Autoencoders for Improved Vision-Language Alignment
stories.author: Bernt Schiele
bullet_points: Introduces TEVI, a framework designed to improve vision-language alignment in models like CLIP.
Addresses the issue of information imbalance where image embeddings contain more data than their text captions describe.
Uses captions as a targeted signal to selectively retain relevant information from image embeddings via Sparse Autoencoders.

stories.title: TailLoR: Protecting Principal Components in Parameter-Efficient Continual Learning
stories.author: Florin Brad
bullet_points: Introduces TailLoR, a new parameter-efficient finetuning method for Continual Learning.
TailLoR uses the singular bases (U and V) of pre-trained weights as a fixed reference frame for low-rank updates.
It employs a soft spectral penalty to prevent updates from interfering with dominant singular directions, enabling fine-grained adaptation.

stories.title: HANDOFF: Humanoid Agentic Task-Space Whole-Body Control via Distilled Complementary Teachers
stories.author: Aaron Ames
bullet_points: Introduces HANDOFF, a new framework for whole-body control in humanoid robots.
Addresses the critical challenge of bridging high-level task planning with low-level robot control.
Proposes a compact, explicit, and intuitive interface to replace traditional dense kinematic references.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection benchmarks, which fail to account for progressive human-AI co-editing.
Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new operation-guided benchmark designed to study how AI authorship signals evolve during revision workflows.
Relevant to the growing integration of AI writing assistants into professional drafting and revision processes.

stories.title: Pretraining Recurrent Networks without Recurrence
stories.author: Phillip Isola
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations of standard Backpropagation Through Time (BPTT) in training Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), specifically regarding sequential processing and gradient issues.
It introduces Supervised Memory Training (SMT), a novel method designed to train nonlinear RNNs.
SMT achieves this by completely bypassing the need for recurrent credit propagation, potentially improving training efficiency and parallelism.

stories.title: RREDCoT: Segment-Level Reward Redistribution for Reasoning Models
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: The paper addresses the delayed reward problem inherent in Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning of reasoning language models.
It introduces RREDCoT, a method for Segment-Level Reward Redistribution, to improve the training process.
The work targets limitations in current methods like GRPO, which treat the entire Chain-of-Thought (CoT) trace as a single unit for reward assignment.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: The research introduces a novel method for self-augmenting retrieval within Diffusion Language Models (DLMs).
DLMs generate text by iteratively denoising responses, predicting tentative tokens at each step.
The discarded, low-confidence tokens from the denoising process are shown to serve as a valuable lookahead signal for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).

stories.title: PC Layer: Polynomial Weight Preconditioning for Improving LLM Pre-Training
stories.author: Ruoyu Sun
bullet_points: Introduces a Polynomial Weight Preconditioning (PC) layer to stabilize LLM training.
The PC module reshapes the singular-value spectrum of weight matrices using low-degree polynomials.
The method introduces no inference overhead as preconditioned weights can be merged back post-training.

stories.title: Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection
stories.author: Zhiqiang Shen
bullet_points: Addresses the gap in current AI-text detection benchmarks, which fail to capture the nuances of human-AI co-editing.
Introduces OpAI-Bench, a new operation-guided benchmark designed to study how AI authorship signals evolve during revision workflows.
Highlights the growing real-world problem of documents being a blend of human and AI contributions, necessitating more sophisticated detection methods.

stories.title: Self-Augmenting Retrieval for Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Kilian Q. Weinberger
bullet_points: The research introduces a novel method for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) using signals from Discrete Diffusion Language Models.
The core finding is that low-confidence, discarded tokens during the denoising process serve as a valuable 'lookahead signal' for retrieval.
This approach leverages the iterative, parallel denoising nature of diffusion models to improve the quality and grounding of generated text.

stories.title: You Only Index Once: Cross-Layer Sparse Attention with Shared Routing
stories.author: Furu Wei
bullet_points: The research addresses the decoding efficiency bottleneck in LLMs, particularly during long-context inference and complex reasoning.
It tackles the existing trade-off between sparse attention methods: structured methods offer speed but lose quality, while token-sparse methods are accurate but lack significant speedup.
The proposed method, 'Cross-Layer Sparse Attention with Shared Routing,' aims to improve both efficiency and quality simultaneously.

stories.title: Scaffold, Not Vocabulary? A Controlled, Two-Tier, Pre-Registered Study of a Popperian Code-Generation Skill
stories.author: Mehmet Iscan
bullet_points: The study investigates the effectiveness of 'prompt skills' (like instructing an LLM to act as a Popperian falsificationist) in improving code generation by Large Language Models.
A key concern addressed is the reliance on LLM-as-a-judge systems, which are known to possess inherent biases (positional, self-preference, stylistic).
The research questions whether observed improvements in code quality are genuinely due to the prompting technique or artifacts of the biased evaluation method.

stories.title: Latent Reasoning with Normalizing Flows
stories.author: Jiatao Gu
bullet_points: The paper proposes 'Latent Reasoning' as an alternative to explicit Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting in LLMs.
Traditional textual CoT forces reasoning through a discrete, serial token stream, which limits computational bandwidth.
Latent reasoning aims to perform intermediate computation in a higher-bandwidth, non-verbalized manner.

stories.title: USAD 2.0: Scaling Representation Distillation for Universal Audio Understanding
stories.author: James Glass
bullet_points: Introduces USAD 2.0, a new universal audio encoder designed to handle diverse audio inputs for LLMs.
Addresses limitations in current multi-domain audio approaches (like USAD and SPEAR) regarding coverage and evaluation.
Explores the integration of knowledge from both self-supervised learning (SSL) and supervised methods for better alignment with audio LLMs.

stories.title: Revising Context, Shifting Simulated Stance: Auditing LLM-Based Stance Simulation in Online Discussions
stories.author: Jiebo Luo
bullet_points: The research addresses the reliability of using LLMs to simulate online user stances in discussions.
It introduces 'counterfactual context revision' as a framework to audit how sensitive these LLM simulations are to context changes.
The study aims to determine if LLM simulations reflect genuine user beliefs or are overly dependent on minor conversational shifts.

stories.title: Run Coding Agents on Local AI — Zero Cloud, Full Control
stories.author: dalenguyen
bullet_points: The article discusses the use of coding agents (like Codex CLI, Claude Code, Cursor, Pi) as productivity tools.
The central theme is running these coding agents locally, eliminating the need for cloud services.
This local execution offers users 'Full Control' over their AI coding tools.

stories.title: The Execution Safety Crisis in Multi-Agent Workflows — And the Architectural Pattern That Solves It
stories.author: vaibhavk289
bullet_points: The article addresses a critical, unresolved issue in multi-agent systems: execution safety.
It posits that execution, rather than reasoning, is the primary bottleneck in current multi-agent workflows.
The piece proposes a specific architectural pattern designed to resolve these execution safety challenges.

stories.title: Governing AI Agents in Codebases Like a Linter
stories.author: serifcolakel
bullet_points: Introduces 'Agent Governance as Code' framework.
Focuses on managing AI agents directly within codebases.
Addresses the need for structured control over autonomous AI agents.
